behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: The behavior is explicitly a static lint rule on template content, so the real failure boundary is parser/rule logic in one component. Integration was considered but rejected because the text says "static prompt-template lint check" (no required module handoff, OS, network, subprocess, or model output).
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function with template fixtures containing copied phase instructions from SKILL.md and assert it rejects with the expected violation locations/codes; include near-miss fixtures to prevent brittle keyword-only matching.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundaries: fixture loading + interpolation + prompt assembly. Unit is too low because leakage can be introduced only after composition. System was considered and rejected: behavior text is about harness assembly, not real network/subprocess/OS-dependent effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt builder pipeline with representative fixtures and interpolation variables, then assert the final assembled prompt has no forbidden phase-instruction fragments or equivalent structural leakage patterns.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on whether model behavior is caused by loaded skill context, not by explicit hard-coded rules. A stubbed model could always pass and hide this causal failure. Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text requires validating behavioral effect from context, which is model-decision dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a live model test where prompt text omits phase rules, load skill context from plugin/skill files via the real loader, and assert observed behavior aligns with that context; confirm removing hard-coded prompt rules does not change pass criteria.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily harness wiring/configuration and data-source substitution across modules (variant selection, file loading, invocation setup). Agentic was considered but rejected because this behavior only requires proving same prompt text can be executed against variants, not proving output quality yet.
behaviors.test_description: Using the real harness, run two invocations with identical user-style prompt text while swapping skill source to baseline vs degraded/missing SKILL.md variant; assert prompt text is byte-identical and only context source changes.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on real model behavior changing when skill context degrades. Mocked/stub outputs would hide the regression this test is designed to catch. Integration was considered but rejected because the asserted failure is behavioral under live model judgment, not just wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run the same user-style prompt with baseline skill context and with degraded/removed SKILL.md section using live model calls; assert baseline passes structural expectations and degraded variant fails those same expectations.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is ordering across components (model client -> capture writer -> parser). Unit is too low because sequencing bugs often appear in orchestration. System was considered but rejected: local artifact persistence/order can be verified without real OS/network-specific behavior beyond normal in-process/file-module integration.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the harness with a response that later fails parsing; assert an artifact containing untouched raw model output is written before parser invocation and remains available for diagnostics.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: This is assertion-engine logic (schema/path/field-placement checks) that can fail within one local validator component. Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text is about constraint semantics, not cross-module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the assertion module with crafted outputs that contain expected keywords but violate schema paths/role placement, and verify structural assertions fail while valid structured outputs pass.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live model execution and regression detection based on model-produced structure. Stubs/keyword fixtures can mask real model drift. Workflow was considered but rejected because this is a single model-evaluation step, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model executions on prompts known to produce semantically similar but structurally drifting outputs; assert structural checks fail where keyword-only checks would pass, demonstrating real-regression detection.
